mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers Buddhism including its history, Theravada and Mahayana schools, the eightfold path, dharma, karma, meditation, and Pure Land, the primary subject of 824.
Presents the life and teachings of Siddhartha Gautama, including the four noble truths, the Deer Park sermon at Sarnath, and the doctrine of rebirth, directly relevant to 824.A.2.
Examines Buddhist ethical practices, the five precepts, and positions on contemporary moral issues, directly relevant to 824.C.3.a on universal ethical and religious practices.
Explores Tibetan Buddhism including the bodhisattva ideal, Tantric practices, the role of the Dalai Lama, and karma doctrine, directly relevant to 824.A.6 and 824.C.2.c on esoteric Buddhism.
Presents Indian philosophical traditions including the four noble truths, dharma, and karma that form the intellectual substrate of Buddhist philosophy, relevant to 824.B.3.
Provides the Hindu cultural and religious context from which Siddhartha Gautama departed, directly relevant to 824.A.1 on Buddhism's background in Hinduism.
Confucianism interacted with Buddhism in China, Korea, and Japan, relevant to 824.A.4-5 on Buddhism's development in East Asia.
Surveys the region where Theravada Buddhism became the dominant religious tradition, directly relevant to 824.A.7 on Buddhism in Ceylon and Southeast Asia.
Examines modern Japan where Zen, Pure Land, and Nichiren Buddhism remain deeply influential, relevant to 824.A.5 and 824.A.8 on Buddhism in the modern era.
Korea has a significant Buddhist tradition shaped by Son (Zen) practice and Confucian interaction, relevant to 824.A.5 on Buddhism in Korea.
The Silk Road served as the primary conduit for Buddhism's transmission from Gandhara to Central Asia and China, relevant to 824.A.4.
Engages with meditative and contemplative traditions that parallel Buddhist practices of mindfulness and insight meditation described in 824.B.4.
Explores ritual practices including passage rites and religious ceremonies, relevant to 824.C.3.c on ceremonies, festivals, and passage rites.
